comment_text:
Loved Georgia part, not so much Ginny`s. Hate that perverted adult script-writers presented 15 - 16 year-old teenagers as sex-obsessed alcoholics. Because they are not. Most of teens at that age dream about being older and doing older stuff, but they are cautious, anxious and prudent (fortunately). But presenting teens as sex-obsessed alcoholics in this show can easily distort the reality of what a teen life is like or should be like. Imagine your grade 10 child and their friends gulping alcohol daily trying to be like one of cool teens in the show. Plus it is creepy to see the what-supposed-to-be-teens having sex. I know that the actors who are playing them are much older, but it still feels creepy. Like the perverted old script-writers are putting their sexual fantasies into life. 

But Georgia part is very good, and the actress is amazing.

comment_text:
It is one of the worst and morally wrong show I have ever seen.

spoiler alert :

So basically in season 3, the killer, Georgia, is freed, and an inocent, Gil, is put in jail. Let me be clear : Gil is a bad person (except with Austin), he is violent and should be punished by law for that. But he should NOT and NEVER be punished for something he is not responsible for, i.e. the murder of Cynthia's husband.
Paul is a good man and an excellent caracter, Georgia only approached him to benefit from being the mayor's wife. Geogia is a manipulator, a liar, a thief, and a killer. She is not a good person, period. Paul should have never ever ever accept someone as her in his life. Mind you, she even lied about having a baby with him. What kind of human do that?!!! It's awful and very bad.
Some people online seem very happy with this ending. I think this show just push radical feminism and "all men are bad, all women are good" ideology further. I hate this ending. I hate manipulation of Justice. This is a shame.

comment_text: I don't normally react to movies or TV in this way - usually, I watch, enjoy and move on.  I am compelled, however, to comment on this show, especially this season.  This, like all teen serials, deal with the normal and sometimes not so normal pangs of growing up, and I have no issue with the way the writers dealt with that here.  It is the adults, specifically Georgia, that concern me.  Simply put, Georgia is a sociopath who has chosen lies, manipulations, tricks and yes, murder to deal with the hardships she has faced.  Sadly, I fear there are many abused Georgias in our society but I can predict that most of them do not resort to murder to solve their problems.  I hesitantly watched this season, hoping that, by the finale, she would either be hospitalized to get help or incarcerated for her crimes.  Rather, the writers have chosen to whitewash what she has done, painting her as a heroic victim who did what she did for her children, her next scam only a scene away.  What's worse, is that she is exonerated because of Ginny forcing her already damaged brother to lie about what he saw.  And the final scene?  More fodder for Georgia to manipulate - a pregnancy.  I understand that show runners want their series to continue for as long as possible, but I no longer have hope that they care enough about society to bring closure that teaches a moral lesson and elevates.  Where are the consequences?  She tells someone - i.e., Joe - that she has committed murder and he just says, "OK!"  It is sad that this show targets teens and the message the writers have sent is that you should do anything - even murder, lie and bully - if you love someone.  Not in my world!

comment_text: It probably won't happen, I can't really see Paul dying, and even though this likely won't happen, I could sort of see Georgia dying, I think it would fit because although the series is called Ginny and Georgia it seems to me that Georgia can never find a happy ending and to be honest I just can't imagine her having a happy ending, I can't imagine her having a bad ending when alive as well. I also feel like, although Ginny would be devastated, I feel as though she might feel somewhat freed by her mothers death if it did happen. I feel, as bad as it is to say that as long as Georgia is alive Ginny can't really find peace, Georgia is chaotic and she brings it wherever she goes, while it would be sad it could mean peace. I feel like it could be the right ending, I can;t really imagine Ginny spending her live an older teen with her mum, it just feels wrong in a way, I feel like as long as Georgia is alive Ginny won't be able to find peace. and honestly I don't think Georgia wants a happy ending, it is never explicitly said that she does, Georgia having a happy ending would mean she would be able to let go, to allow softness which just isn't her. It doesn't look like Georgia can find peace, that's not her normalcy. As bad as it feels to say we could say that Georgia deserves, she has killed, lied and stole, and while some were accidental and for a could cause she still did it, she had the option not to choose murder and she chose it.
